title: Daily Briefing — Jun 16
headline: Tech's showing a rebound but flows and rotation are warning: patience, don't chase.
body: Right, big picture: Markets are bouncing with SPY up 1.5% at $752.93, and QQQ holding above the weekly 20 MA. Fear & Greed is at 40.5 — that's still in the 'fear' zone, so there's no overcrowding, but we've come off extreme greed last month. Volatility is easing; VIX is down to 16.2 and trending below its 20-day average, so the panic phase is long gone.

Rotation-wise, XLK (tech) stays strong but momentum is weakening, while Materials (XLB), Industrials (XLI), and Utilities (XLU) are improving. Financials (XLF) and Energy (XLE) are lagging with high risk — notably, XLE dropped 4.1% and SM got crushed (-9%). That’s a classic rollover signal. Defensive sectors (Staples, Utilities) are picking up, confirming a shift away from frothy growth.

Contrarian note: Tech names like AAPL, MSFT, and NVDA are rallying but still below their daily 20 MA — do NOT chase here (R001, R011). Retail hasn’t piled in, but flows in Energy and Space stocks have broken down hard. This looks eerily like past cycle tops; leadership is narrowing and laggards are rolling over. If it's in the press, it's already in the price — wait for a proper mean reversion or dark pool accumulation before stepping in. Abundance mindset.
callout_body:
SPY: $752.93 (watch daily/weekly 20 MA)
QQQ: $735.88 (above SMA20 $724.75)
AAPL: $298.24 (below SMA20 $303.78)
NVDA: $209.14 (watch SMA20 $213.66)
XLE: $55.21 (breakdown, SMA20 $58.13)
XLF: $54.21 (lagging)
XLB: $52.73 (momentum improving)
HYG: $80.03 (bull, above all MAs — bonds calming)
action_items: Do NOT chase tech laggards until daily close > 20 MA (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA) — wait for the flows.
Watch for further breakdown in Energy and Space (XLE, SM, PL, LUNR) for defensive rotation plays — consider Staples, Utilities, Materials as rotational winners.
closing_quote: Less trades = more wins. If bonds freak out, everything breaks — but right now, patience and react, don't predict.
